title: alnus glutinosa var. emarginata
intro: Alnus glutinosa var. emarginata, also known as the Emarginate Alder, is a variant of the black alder tree that is native to certain regions in Europe. This plant is used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial qualities, which can help soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The Emarginate Alder is believed to have various health benefits, including lowering blood pressure, reducing fever, and acting as an antiseptic to promote healing and prevent infection. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infused oil, which can be used topically to treat skin irritations and wounds, or taken internally to alleviate respiratory issues and improve overall well-being.
